q.en: In 2001, Ichiro joined which Major League Baseball team, becoming the first non-pitcher from Japan to play in the United States?
q.zh: 2001年，一郎加入了哪支美国职业棒球大联盟球队，成为首位在美国效力的日本非投手球员？

explain.en: The book says, "Ichiro joined the Seattle Mariners in 2001, becoming the first non-pitcher from Japan to play in the United States." He later moved to the Yankees in 2012.
explain.zh: 书中写道：“2001年一郎加入了西雅图水手队，成为首位在美国效力的日本非投手球员。”他后来在2012年转投洋基队。

q.en: On October 1, 2004, Ichiro made his 258th hit and broke baseball's single-season hits record. Whose record did he break?
q.zh: 2004年10月1日，一郎打出了第258支安打，打破了棒球单赛季安打纪录。他打破的是谁的纪录？

explain.en: The book explains, "Ichiro finally broke the record for single hits in a season that had been set by George Sisler in 1920."
explain.zh: 书中解释道：“一郎最终打破了由乔治·西斯勒在1920年创下的单赛季安打纪录。”

q.en: The book tells us, "Ichiro, which means 'first boy,' learned well." According to the story, what does the name Ichiro mean?
q.zh: 书中写道：“一郎，意思是‘第一个男孩’，学得很好。”根据故事，一郎这个名字是什么意思？

explain.en: The text directly states the meaning: "Ichiro, which means 'first boy,' learned well."
explain.zh: 文中直接说明了含义：“一郎，意思是‘第一个男孩’，学得很好。”

q.en: As a glossary word, "rookie" is defined in the book as a person in his or her first year in a sports league. In 2001, Ichiro was a rookie who won two big awards. Which two?
q.zh: 书中把“rookie（新秀）”定义为在某体育联盟第一个年头的运动员。2001年一郎作为新秀赢得了两个大奖，是哪两个？

options.en: Rookie of the Year and Most Valuable Player (MVP)
options.zh: 年度最佳新秀奖和最有价值球员奖（MVP）

options.en: World Series and Rookie of the Year
options.zh: 世界大赛奖和年度最佳新秀奖

explain.en: The book says, "Ichiro was so good that he won the American League Rookie of the Year Award. He also won the Most Valuable Player Award (MVP). A rookie player rarely wins both."
explain.zh: 书中说：“一郎非常出色，赢得了美国联盟年度最佳新秀奖，还赢得了最有价值球员奖（MVP）。新秀球员极少能同时赢得这两个奖项。”

q.en: The book includes a career statistics table called "Ichiro by the Numbers." According to that table, what was Ichiro's total number of career hits?
q.zh: 书中包含一个名为“一郎的数据”的职业生涯统计表。根据该表，一郎职业生涯安打总数是多少？

explain.en: The career table lists "Total ... 2,606" hits across his time with Seattle and the Yankees.
explain.zh: 职业生涯统计表列出他在水手队和洋基队期间的安打“总计……2,606”支。

q.en: Why did Ichiro's father make his right-handed son bat from the left side of the plate?
q.zh: 为什么一郎的父亲让这个惯用右手的儿子从本垒板的左侧击球？

options.en: So Ichiro would be closer to first base

options.zh: 因为左手击球手力量更大

options.en: So he could hit more home runs
options.zh: 这样他能打更多全垒打

explain.en: The book states, "His father also made his right-handed son bat from the left side of the plate. That way, Ichiro would be closer to first base."
explain.zh: 书中说：“父亲还让这个惯用右手的儿子从本垒板左侧击球。这样，一郎离一垒更近。”

q.en: After breaking the record, Ichiro bowed to George Sisler's 81-year-old daughter, who was sitting near first base. What was the author's purpose in including this moment?
q.zh: 打破纪录后，一郎向坐在靠近一垒处、81岁的西斯勒的女儿鞠躬。作者描写这一刻的目的是什么？

options.en: To show Ichiro's respect and good sportsmanship
options.zh: 为了展现一郎的敬意和良好的体育精神

options.en: To explain how baseball rules work

options.zh: 为了证明西斯勒很生气
为了描述体育场的布局

explain.en: The book says, "Ichiro bowed to her, a sign of respect in Japan," showing the author wanted to highlight his respectful character.
explain.zh: 书中写道：“一郎向她鞠躬，这是日本表示敬意的方式”，表明作者想突出他尊敬他人的品格。

q.en: While playing in the United States, how many Gold Gloves did Ichiro win for his great defensive play?
q.zh: 在美国打球期间，一郎凭借出色的防守赢得了多少个金手套奖？

explain.en: The book states, "He also won ten Gold Gloves for great defensive play."
explain.zh: 书中写道：“他还凭借出色的防守赢得了十个金手套奖。”

q.en: The book notes Ichiro was "thinner and smaller than most American players" and "didn't hit the ball hard. Instead, he slapped at it." What does the author show by describing this?
q.zh: 书中提到一郎“比大多数美国球员更瘦小”，而且“击球并不用力，而是轻轻拨球”。作者这样描写是为了说明什么？

options.en: That skill, speed, and hard work helped him succeed despite his size
options.zh: 尽管身材瘦小，技巧、速度和努力仍让他获得成功

options.en: That only big players can win in baseball
options.zh: 只有身材高大的球员才能在棒球中获胜

options.en: That he was a poor hitter
That Japan plays a different sport
options.zh: 日本玩的是另一种运动

explain.en: The book contrasts his small size with his huge success, noting he "led the league in singles ten years in a row," to show perseverance beats size.
explain.zh: 书中将他的瘦小身材与巨大成功作对比，指出他“连续十年领跑联盟一垒安打”，以说明毅力胜过身材。

q.en: At the end, the author calls Ichiro "one of the most feared hitters in baseball" and writes, "Not too bad for a skinny guy." What is the author's main purpose here?
q.zh: 在结尾，作者称一郎是“棒球界最令人畏惧的击球手之一”，并写道“对一个瘦小的家伙来说还不赖”。作者这里的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inspire readers by showing that size does not limit success
options.zh: 通过展示身材并不限制成功来激励读者

explain.en: The author closes by celebrating how far a "skinny guy" came, aiming to inspire readers with his story of perseverance.
explain.zh: 作者以赞美这个“瘦小的家伙”取得的成就作结，旨在用他持之以恒的故事激励读者。

prompt.en: Ichiro practiced up to four hours a day as a child and became one of baseball's greatest hitters. What lesson about hard work and perseverance can you take from his story?
prompt.zh: 一郎小时候每天练习长达四个小时，最终成为棒球史上最伟大击球手之一。从他的故事中，你能学到关于努力和坚持的什么道理？

en: His father made him practice up to four hours a day, every day, year round.
In high school he woke at 3:00 A.M. to do chores before playing ball and studying.
As a rookie in 2001 he won both Rookie of the Year and the MVP award.
In 2004 he broke George Sisler's 1920 record with his 258th hit of the season.
zh: 他的父亲让他每天练习长达四个小时，全年无休。
上高中时，他凌晨3点起床做完家务，再去打球和学习。
2001年作为新秀，他同时赢得了年度最佳新秀奖和MVP奖。
2004年，他以赛季第258支安打打破了西斯勒1920年创下的纪录。
